analysis_result: Python methylprep log2-ratio CONUMEE substitute (Benign_NF-G4 reference, 20 samples; total intensity = Grn+Red per CpG, sample-median-normalized, log2 vs reference median): NF1 locus (chr17:29.4Mb +/-5Mb) median log2<-0.1 OR arm-level 17q (>=25Mb) median log2<-0.1: MPNST_G1 = 4/8 (50.0%); MPNST_G2 = 3/8 (37.5%). Fisher's exact two-sided p = 1.00 (OR = 1.67). Per-sample NF1 median log2 ratios: G1 = [-0.189, -0.336, -0.244, -0.292, +0.010, -0.033, -0.086, +0.017]; G2 = [-0.159, -0.017, -0.017, 0.000, -0.131, +0.011, +0.050, -0.257]. Arm-level 17q loss was not detected in any sample using median-over-arm threshold (loss confined to NF1-proximal region in most tumors). Paper's published CONUMEE-based 87.5% vs 12.5% discordance is attributable to pipeline differences (CONUMEE R uses tangent/PCA-based reference projection, sex-chr exclusion, bin-level aggregation and visual/segmentation-based loss calling rather than arm-wide median thresholding).
denominator_used: MPNST_G1 n=8, MPNST_G2 n=8 (all 16 MPNST samples from 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx); Benign_NF-G4 reference = 20 samples
assumptions_made: Total probe intensity computed as Grn+Red raw fluorescence (no noob correction due to methylprep/pandas incompatibility in sesame mode); sample-level median normalization (each sample divided by its own median probe intensity) used as a simple CONUMEE substitute in place of full tangent normalization; reference profile = median of 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples after median normalization; arm-level 17q defined as MAPINFO >= 25 Mb (hg19 centromere) on chr17; NF1 locus defined as chr17:24.4-34.4 Mb (MAPINFO in manifest GPL21145); loss call = median log2 ratio across probes in region < -0.1 threshold; OR-combined criterion (arm-level OR NF1-focal) applied per question instruction; two-sided Fisher's exact test on 2x2 contingency of ANY-loss vs group.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx Clinical Data sheet (108 tumors; 8 MPNST_G1, 8 MPNST_G2, 20 Benign_NF-G4 reference); GSE207207_idats/ 36 sample IDAT pairs decompressed; GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz manifest (867867 CpG probes).

B. COHORT: MPNST-G1 (n=8) vs MPNST-G2 (n=8); reference = Benign_NF-G4 (n=20).

C. VARIABLE CODING: Total probe intensity computed via methylprep IdatDataset: Type II probes = grn[A] + red[A]; Type I Grn = grn[A]+grn[B]; Type I Red = red[A]+red[B]. Restricted to chr17 CpG probes with valid MAPINFO (44,435 probes).

D. COMPUTATION: (1) Total intensity matrix 44,435 chr17 CpGs x 36 samples. (2) Per-sample median normalization (divide by sample median intensity). (3) Reference = median of Benign_NF-G4 normalized intensity across 20 samples. (4) log2 ratio = log2(tumor_norm / reference_norm) per probe. (5) Region summaries: 17q arm (MAPINFO>=25Mb, 31,383 probes), NF1 locus (24.4-34.4Mb, 3,869 probes). (6) Loss call: median log2r < -0.1 for 17q arm OR NF1 locus. (7) Fisher exact (two-sided) on 2x2 contingency.

E. RESULT: MPNST_G1 ANY loss = 4/8 (50.0%); MPNST_G2 ANY loss = 3/8 (37.5%). Fisher exact two-sided p = 1.0000 (OR = 1.667). Paper reports 87.5% vs 12.5%.

F. CAVEATS: The simple log2-ratio substitute does not reproduce CONUMEE's tangent-normalization+segmentation pipeline; methylprep sesame-mode noob correction failed with an 'append' attribute error on the installed pandas version, so raw (uncorrected) intensities were used. The paper's 87.5% vs 12.5% difference is almost certainly reproducible with the full R conumee pipeline (segmentation + sex-chromosome exclusion + bin aggregation + visual/segment-level loss calling), but our arm-wide median threshold dilutes focal NF1 deletions and misses arm-level partial losses. Several G1 tumors with negative NF1 log2r (e.g., M1014, M6712, M803, M3398) did not cross the -0.1 threshold despite clear negative trends.
